backend/main.mo: Motoko actor defining an Order type and three public functions: placeOrder (stores a new order and returns its ID), getOrder (retrieves a single order by ID), and listOrders (returns all orders).
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report confirming that the website name 'Crown Followers' has been applied to the browser tab title, Header, and Footer across the relevant frontend files.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries for each frontend source file, serving as a quick reference for the project's frontend structure and contents.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base stylesheet defining CSS custom properties for light and dark color themes using OKLCH tokens, applied globally to the body and border elements.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point that sets the browser tab title to 'Crown Followers' and mounts the React app via the root div and main.tsx module script.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that composes the full page layout by rendering Header, Hero, ServicesSection, OrderForm, PaymentSection, Footer, and the floating WhatsAppButton.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: Footer component displaying the 'Crown Followers' brand name, a services list with smooth-scroll links, and contact details including WhatsApp and UPI ID.
frontend/src/components/Header.tsx: Sticky header component featuring the 'Crown Followers' brand logo with a Crown icon, desktop and mobile navigation links with smooth scrolling, and a WhatsApp CTA button.
frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx: Full-screen hero section with a background image, animated floating stat cards for Instagram and YouTube metrics, a brand logo, headline, and CTA buttons linking to services and order sections.
frontend/src/components/OrderForm.tsx: React component that renders an order form for social media services, handles service selection via custom events, submits orders via a mutation hook, and shows a confirmation screen with WhatsApp redirect on success.
frontend/src/components/PaymentSection.t: Displays UPI payment instructions including a QR code, copyable UPI ID, and step-by-step payment guide for completing orders via WhatsApp screenshot confirmation.
Renders a tabbed services/pricing section with cards for Instagram and YouTube packages, dispatching a custom event to scroll to and pre-select a service in the order form.
frontend/src/components/WhatsAppButton.t: Fixed floating WhatsApp chat button that links to a pre-filled WhatsApp message for contacting the service provider.
frontend/src/data/pricingData.ts: Defines TypeScript interfaces and static pricing data for all available Instagram and YouTube social media service packages, including tiered and flat package structures.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor using the current Internet Identity, invalidating dependent queries whenever the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and provider using DFINITY's AuthClient, exposing login/logout functions and status flags for use throughout the app.
frontend/src/hooks/useOrderForm.ts: Custom React hook managing order form state (service, package, profile URL, price, WhatsApp) with helpers for validation, WhatsApp message generation, and form reset.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Provides React Query hooks for placing an order (mutation) and listing all orders (query) via the ICP actor.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file importing Google Fonts and Tailwind directives, defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for a dark gold-themed design system with utility classes.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that mounts the app wrapped in QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the theme with custom OKLCH color tokens (gold, surface, whatsapp, sidebar), fonts, shadows, and keyframe animations.
project_state.json: Project metadata tracking the 'Crown Followers' social media service platform state, including existing features, pending rename request, known issues, and architectural notes.
